channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Anti-DOGE protests at Tesla stores target Elon Musk's bottom line
item.description: Demonstrators gathered outside Tesla stores across the U.S. Saturday to protest the automaker's billionaire CEO, Elon Musk, and his push to slash government spending on behalf of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Lifelong New York civil rights advocate and NAACP leader Hazel Dukes dies at 92
item.description: Hazel Dukes, the president of the New York State chapter of the NAACP and lifelong civil rights advocate, died Saturday at the age of 92. Dukes peacefully passed away in her New York City home surrounded by family, her son, Ronald Dukes, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order designating English as the official language of the US
item.description: President Donald Trump signed on Saturday an executive order designating English as the official language of the United States.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge extends block on Trump gender-affirming care federal funding ban
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge Lauren King ruled that President Trump’s executive orders violate the Constitution’s Fifth Amendment, which grants equal protection under the law.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, ABC 4

item.title: David Johansen, singer from the seminal punk band the New York Dolls, dies at 75
item.description: Johansen died Friday at his home in New York City, Jeff Kilgour, a family spokesperson told The Associated Press. It was revealed in early 2025 that he had stage 4 cancer and a brain tumor.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: Cuomo jumps into New York mayor's race, attempting political comeback
item.description: Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo (D) announced Saturday afternoon that he's entering New York City’s mayoral race, adding a big name to the already busy Democratic primary challenging Mayor Eric Adams (D).
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Lee Kunzman, 'persistent' Indy 500 veteran, dies at 80
item.description: Kunzman's best finish in his INDYCAR SERIES career was in 1979, when he placed second at Atlanta Motor Speedway. It was just a narrow loss to winner Johnny Rutherford, IMS officials said.
item.dc:creator: Cooper Hudson, NewsNation

item.title: Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo launches political comeback with a run for New York City mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former New York Gov. Andrew Cuomo announced Saturday that he is running for mayor of New York City, relaunching his political career following a yearslong exile over a barrage of sexual harassment accusations.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: 2 window washers rescued from 'spinning' scaffolding in Manhattan
item.description: The wind took over a scaffolding while two workers were washing the windows of a high-rise building in Midtown.
item.dc:creator: Eliecer Marte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FedEx plane catches fire midair before making emergency landing
item.description: A Fedex cargo caught fire midair and then made a dramatic emergency landing at an airport official told Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE arrest of migrant sparks anger protest before violent gang ties exposed
item.description: An illegal migrant arrested while dropping students off at a Chicago school sparked outrage but that was before ICE revealed his violent gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas measles cases rise to 146 in an outbreak that led to a child's death
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The number of people with measles in Texas increased to 146 in an outbreak that led this week to the death of an unvaccinated school-aged child, health officials said Friday. The number of cases — Texas’ largest in nearly 30 years — increased by 22 since Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KRON4

item.title: US lobster catch drops as crustaceans migrate to colder Canadian waters
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — The U.S. lobster industry's catch keeps sliding as fishermen contend with the northward migration of the valuable crustaceans. The industry is based mostly in Maine, where lobsters are both a cultural signifier and the backbone of the coastal economy.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Students escape school bus fire in Ohio
item.description: An emergency broke out Thursday morning on a school bus carrying children in Cleveland Heights, Ohio. An unexpected blaze — requiring a quick evacuation.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Israel influencer says Bibas memorial in NYC 'brought out the worst' in antisemites
item.description: Pro-Israel influencer Zach Sage Fox set out to honor Ariel and Kfir Bibas with a memorial in Times Square, but he says he was met with "some of the worst antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Economic blackout protest underway Friday
item.description: (AP) — A grassroots organization is encouraging U.S. residents not to spend any money Friday as an act of “economic resistance” to protest what the group's founder sees as the malign influence of billionaires, big corporations and both major political parties on the lives of working Americans.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Gene Hackman was more than an everyman: An Appreciation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — One of the greatest American actors of the 20th century was voted “least likely to succeed” by his first theater school, wasn’t a star until he was 40 and possessed a face he once described as “your everyday mineworker.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in a new memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government is facing a generational realignment as President Donald Trump directs federal agencies to develop plans for eliminating employee positions and consolidating programs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found in these states as death toll climbs
item.description: A listeria outbreak tied to supplement shakes is now linked to at least 12 deaths and 38 cases across 21 states, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas says this doctor illegally treated trans youth. He says he followed the law
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (AP) — On the Texas border, Dr. Hector Granados treats children with diabetes at his El Paso clinics and makes hospital rounds under the shadow of accusations that have thrown his career into jeopardy: providing care to transgender youth. In what's believed to be a U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Navy sailor pleads guilty in plot to attack Naval Station Great Lakes, allegedly on behalf of Iranian group, feds say
item.description: NORTH CHICAGO — A former U.S. Navy sailor faces two decades in prison after pleading guilty to plotting to attack Naval Station Great Lakes in North Chicago, allegedly on behalf of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in order to avenge the death of one of its leaders.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigations
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman arrested, charged after allegedly locking boyfriend in storage unit for 5 days
item.description: Investigators believe she asked her boyfriend to crawl toward the back of the "hoarder"-like storage unit to retrieve an item, then locked him inside.
item.dc:creator: Abby Mittower, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5
